methods.description: TFOT is used to simulate the short-term oxidative ageing of asphalt mastics. It involves heating the mastic in an iron pan at a specified temperature and duration to assess ageing effects.

steps.description: The mastic with the amount 50 g was placed in an iron pan.
Then, iron pan was placed on the tray. The axis of revolution was vertical.
steps.searchable_summary: place iron pan on vertical axis tray

steps.description: The ageing was conducted at 163 ∘ C for 5 h.

methods.description: This method evaluates the high temperature susceptibility of asphalt by determining the temperature at which asphalt can no longer support a steel ball.

steps.description: Softening point is the temperature that asphalt could not support the steel ball with the weight 3.5 g at the heating rate 5 ∘ C/min according to ASTM D 36.
steps.searchable_summary: determine softening point using ASTM D 36

steps.description: Increment of softening point (Δ) often was used to evaluate the ageing degree.

methods.description: This test evaluates the consistency of asphalt by measuring its penetration at various temperatures before and after TFOT.

steps.description: Penetrations of asphalt mastics were tested at 15, 25, and 30 ∘ C according to ASTM D 5 before and after TFOT.
steps.searchable_summary: measure penetration at specified temperatures using ASTM D 5

steps.description: Percent retained penetration (PRP) was used to evaluate the antiageing effects.

methods.description: This test evaluates the tensile cohesive property of asphalt mastics by measuring maximum load and force ductility curves.

steps.description: Samples were prepared with the mold of the elastic recovery test to produce a test specimen with constant cross section.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples with elastic recovery mold

steps.description: The maximum load and force ductility curves were recorded during the test conducted at 5 ∘ C.
steps.searchable_summary: record maximum load and force ductility curves

steps.description: The force ductility curve could be divided into two parts, and data of the first part was used to analyze the effect of ageing.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze first part of force ductility curve

steps.description: The ageing effect on tensile cohesive property could be reflected by the ductility retention rate (DRR) and deformation energy ageing index (JAI).
